rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash description ('Arrearagia Recipienda Nov^r 2. 1762.') for accuracy confirmed visually, as it precisely matches the script. Both models agreed on header type and empty amounts. gpt-5-mini had 'Arrearages Received Novr. 2. 1762'.
This explicit column header row ('£: S: D:') was correctly identified by gpt-5-mini and confirmed visually. gemini-flash missed this row entirely. Added as a missing row.

Selected gemini-flash values (£5 11s 0d) and description ('pro annis'). Visually, the pounds are '05', shillings '11', and pence a dash (0). gpt-5-mini misread the amounts as £0 5s 11d and description as 'pro anno'.
rows.english_description: Annual rent due from the rectory of Merton.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent due from the rectory of Merton, amounting to £5 11s 0d.

rows.notes: Full consensus on description and all monetary values. Visually confirmed as accurate: £47 1s 5¾d.
rows.english_description: Rent from Chase Hill (arrears received).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Chase Hill, amounting to £47 1s 5 3/4d.

rows.notes: Selected gpt-5-mini's pence value (5¼d), which is clearly visible with a small quarter-farthing symbol, overriding gemini-flash's '0d'. Descriptions and other amounts were consistent between models.

rows.notes: Selected gpt-5-mini's pence value (1½d) and description ('Tenem.') as both are more accurate visually. The pence clearly show '1' followed by a halfpenny symbol. The word appears to be 'Tenem.' (for tenement). Pounds and shillings were consistent.
